channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Mobile AI opens new horizons for sustainable business growth in the digital age
item.description: As we navigate through 2024 and gear up for 2025, we are witnessing a pivotal moment in technological evolution where two transformative forces—5.5G (also referred to as 5G-Advanced or 5G-A) networks and artificial intelligence (AI)—are converging to reshape our digital landscape.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How a wrong Google search can compromise your data and bring law enforcement calling
item.description: Wrong Google searches can not only compromise your device and personal data. They can also bring law enforcement to your doorstep. Hackers are targeting Google searches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Chinese humanoid robot shows off its strength by lifting 35 pounds per hand
item.description: China's Shanghai Kepler Robotics has developed a new humanoid robot that can carry up to 35 pounds per hand for commercial applications across various industries
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple’s AirPods Pro 2 makes hearing tests as easy as a few taps
item.description: Checking your hearing has gotten easier with the new hearing test feature in AirPods Pro 2. You can take reliable hearing tests using your iPhone or iPad.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New framework aims to keep AI safe in US critical infrastructure
item.description: Analyst reaction to Thursday’s release by the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) of a framework designed to ensure safe and secure deployment of AI in critical infrastructure is decidedly mixed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Lawsuit alleging Elon Musk rigged Dogecoin is dropped
item.description: A lawsuit accusing Elon Musk of rigging Dogecoin is ending. Investors in the cryptocurrency who said the world’s richest person and his electric car company Tesla committed fraud and insider trading are withdrawing their appeal from an Aug. 29 dismissal of their case.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Go language evolving for future hardware, AI workloads
item.description: The Go programming language having just turned 15 years old on November 10, proponents now are planning to adapt the Go language to large multicore systems, the latest vector and matrix hardware instructions, and the needs of AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 21 proposed features.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: FBI probe into Chinese hacking finds 'broad and significant' espionage campaign
item.description: The U.S. government's ongoing probe into China-backed actors' attempts to hack into U.S. telecommunications infrastructure revealed a "broad and significant" espionage campaign targeted at those in government or political work, the FBI announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: In Cyprus, Ukrainians learn how to dispose of landmines that kill and maim hundreds
item.description: NICOSIA, Cyprus (AP) — In a Cypriot National Guard camp, Ukrainians are being trained on how to identify, locate and dispose of landmines and other unexploded munitions that litter huge swaths of their country, killing and maiming hundreds of people, including children.
item.dc:creator: MENELAOS HADJICOSTIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meet the floating Android note app of the future
item.description: Time for a brutal truth any productivity connoisseur can confirm: At a certain point, once you’ve seen one note-taking app, you’ve kinda seen ’em all. I mean, sure: We’ve got some commendably compelling choices when it comes to note-taking apps on Android.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Berkeley councilmembers approve controversial tax cut for innovation industry start-ups
item.description: The measure is meant to help start-up research and development companies by allowing them to focus their limited grant dollars on their projects.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Lopez, The Mercury News

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: OpenAI’s SimpleQA tool for discerning genAI accuracy — right message, wrong messenger
item.description: In the ongoing and potentially futile effort by CIOs to squeeze meaningful ROI out of their shiny, new generative AI (genAI) tools, there is no more powerful villain than hallucinations. It is what causes everyone to seriously wonder whether the analysis genAI delivers is valid and usable.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What the Trump election means for Microsoft’s AI dreams
item.description: With last week’s US elections, everything changed for the tech industry, especially for Microsoft. Given President-elect Donald J.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bitcoin neared $90,000 in a new record high. What to know about crypto's post-election rally
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As money continues to pour into crypto following Donald Trump's reelection last week, bitcoin has climbed to yet another record high. The world's largest cryptocurrency topped $89,000 for the first time, briefly peaking at $89,995 early Tuesday, according to CoinDesk.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cisco launches intelligent Wi-Fi 7 access points
item.description: Cisco has taken the wraps off a pair of intelligent WiFi-7 access points and introduced a new way of licensing wireless gear across cloud, on-premises and hybrid networks.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 5 Linux commands for measuring disk activity
item.description: Ever wonder how hard your disks are working? If so, you’re in luck. Linux provides quite a few useful commands for gauging disk activity. This post examines five options that are all very useful and easy to use.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Facebook and Instagram users in Europe can opt for less personalized ads
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Facebook and Instagram users in Europe will get the option to see less personalized ads if they don't want to pay for an ad-free subscription, social media company Meta said Tuesday, bowing to pressure from Brussels over privacy and digital competition concerns.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: VMware by Broadcom: Product, service and support news
item.description: News of Broadcom’s plans to acquire VMware triggered concerns among customers, partners, and industry watchers, many of whom recalled Broadcom’s track record with prior high-profile acquisitions, including network switching vendor Brocade in 2016 for almost $6 billion, software firm CA Technologies
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Snowflake bares its agentic AI plans by showcasing its Intelligence platform
item.description: As enterprises look to automate more processes with the help of AI-based agents that don’t require human intervention, Snowflake has showcased a new offering at its ongoing developer conference, Build 2024, which it claims is its agentic AI proposition for enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Prada's out-of-this-world spacesuit for NASA
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how Axiom Space and luxury designer Prada are collaborating on NASA's Artemis III spacesuit design.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Why your AI models stumble before the finish line
item.description: In 2023, enterprises across industries invested heavily in generative AI proof of concepts (POCs), eager to explore the technology’s potential. Fast-forward to 2024, companies face a new challenge: moving AI initiatives from prototype to production.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Avoiding Siri slipups and apologies for butt dials
item.description: Voice assistants may cause confusion across devices. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers some solutions to fix it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
